# charts

<!-- archie:ai-start -->

> Organisational parent for the two self-contained Helm charts (benthos-collector, openmeter) that package all OpenMeter Kubernetes workloads. Owns only the shared helm-docs generation pipeline (Makefile + template.md) consumed by both child charts; no shared K8s manifests, CRDs, or cluster-scoped RBAC live at this level.

## Patterns

**helm-docs via shared template** — Both child chart READMEs are generated by helm-docs from the shared template.md defined here. Run `make docs` to regenerate; never hand-edit a chart README.md. (`make docs  # helm-docs -s file -c . -t $PWD/template.md -t README.tmpl.md`)
**OCI Helm registry distribution** — Charts are packaged with `helm package deploy/charts/<CHART> --version <VERSION> --destination build/helm` and pushed to ghcr.io/openmeterio/helm-charts/. Chart.yaml version and appVersion stay aligned with the container image tag. (`helm package deploy/charts/openmeter --version 1.2.3 --destination build/helm`)
**Self-contained child charts** — Each child chart (benthos-collector, openmeter) is fully self-contained with its own templates/, values.yaml, Chart.yaml, and README.tmpl.md. No shared manifests live at deploy/charts level. (`# deploy/charts/openmeter/templates/  vs  deploy/charts/benthos-collector/templates/`)

## Key Files

| File | Role | Watch For |
|------|------|-----------|
| `Makefile` | Runs helm-docs across both child charts using the shared template.md. The sole target is `make docs`. | helm-docs must be on PATH (it is in the Nix CI shell); running outside Nix silently produces no output if helm-docs is missing. |
| `template.md` | Shared Go-template for helm-docs defining badge helpers (versionBadge, typeBadge, appVersionBadge, kubeVersionBadge, artifactHubBadge) and the tldr / chart.base layout used by both child README templates. | Editing badge/section templates affects both child READMEs. artifactHubBadge URL is derived from .Name — renaming a chart breaks its ArtifactHub listing. |

## Anti-Patterns

- Hand-editing a child chart README.md — generated by helm-docs from template.md and values.yaml comments; edits are overwritten on next `make docs`
- Adding a third chart at this level without a corresponding README.tmpl.md and updating the Makefile docs target
- Storing shared Kubernetes manifests (CRDs, cluster-scoped RBAC) at deploy/charts level — each chart must be fully self-contained
- Publishing charts without aligning Chart.yaml appVersion with the container image tag used in values.yaml
- Adding optional infrastructure to the openmeter chart without both an enabled guard in the resource template AND a matching override block in configmap.yaml

## Decisions

- **Shared template.md at deploy/charts level rather than duplicated per chart** — Both charts share the same badge set and TL;DR install pattern; centralising ensures consistent branding and eliminates duplication across helm-docs runs.
- **OCI registry (ghcr.io) as the distribution channel, not a traditional Helm repo index** — OCI registries are natively supported by Helm 3+ and reuse the same GHCR credentials as container images, simplifying the release pipeline.
- **All OpenMeter binaries share one image and tag in the openmeter chart** — The mono-repo produces all binaries from a single Dockerfile and release tag; sharing one image tag enforces version consistency and prevents partial upgrades (only Svix is exempt with its own image fields).

## Example: Add a new OpenMeter workload to the openmeter chart

```
# templates/my-worker.yaml
{{- if .Values.myWorker.enabled }}
# name:   {{ include "openmeter.componentName" (list . "my-worker") }}
# labels: {{ include "openmeter.componentLabels" (list . "my-worker") | nindent 4 }}
# pod annotation so config changes trigger rollouts:
#   checksum/config: {{ include (print $.Template.BasePath "/configmap.yaml") . | sha256sum }}
# reuse Values.image.repository / Values.image.tag — never add a separate image field
{{- end }}
# then: helm lint deploy/charts/openmeter
```

<!-- archie:ai-end -->
